explanation: This command simulates a malicious registration of the TacticalRMM agent to an attacker-controlled server. It includes all required flags (--api, --auth, --client-id, --site-id, --agent-type) as specified in the Sigma rule, making it a valid test case for detection.
Same as the first command; no mutation is needed because the Sigma rule is based on the presence of specific command-line arguments. This is a duplicate for completeness.
Reordered the arguments (--client-id and --site-id) to test if reordering can bypass detection. The command remains valid and should still be detected by the Sigma rule.
Used double quotes around the executable path to test insertion of quotes as a mutation technique. The Sigma rule should still detect this command.
Used a long-form path with escaped backslashes to test path normalization. The Sigma rule should still detect this command.
